tools.name: AdFind
tools.evidence_snippet: Used to query and retrieve information from Active Directory.

tools.name: Bloodhound
tools.evidence_snippet: Used to query and retrieve information from Active Directory.

tools.name: GMER
tools.evidence_snippet: A software tool intended to be used for detecting and removing rootkits.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.evidence_snippet: A tool designed to run programs and execute commands on remote systems.

tools.name: WinPEAS
tools.evidence_snippet: Used to search for additional privilege escalation paths.

tools.name: Microsoft Nltest
tools.evidence_snippet: Used by Play ransomware actors for network discovery.

tools.name: Nekto / PriviCMD
tools.evidence_snippet: Used by Play ransomware actors for privilege escalation.

ttps.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems to gain access to networks.
Play ransomware actors use tools like GRIXBA to identify network configurations and settings.
Play ransomware actors scan for antivirus software.
Play ransomware actors use tools like Mimikatz to dump credentials.

ttps.technique: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility
ttps.evidence_snippet: Play ransomware actors use tools like WinRAR to compress files.

summary: This joint Cybersecurity Advisory is part of an ongoing #StopRansomware effort to publish advisories for network defenders that detail various ransomware variants and ransomware threat actors.
